parsed_review.recommendation: Approve CP00-126 closeout. The pack closes the planned Risk B failure taxonomy workflow and permission/audit binding range RP02.P07.M03.S21-RP02.P07.M05.S16 as synthetic, metadata-only, no-write references with no runtime surface added.
parsed_review.rationale: Scope and counts are internally consistent and self-validated. buildRowsFromPhases produces 40 rows across three micro-phases (M03=2 primary review/escalation, M04=22 secondary workflow, M05=16 permission/audit binding opening slice); first/last unit ids resolve to RP02.P07.M03.S21 and RP02.P07.M05.S16, unit ids are unique, and deliverable/domain/phase counts match the assertions in validatePermissionKernelCp126Coverage and the script (failure_recovery 24, implementation 4, security_audit 3, hermes_evidence 3, fixture 2, test 2, claude_review 2). Claude edge-case prompt rows return claude_edge_case_prompt.read_only=true and executes_claude_review=false (top-level executes_claude_review=false), and routeForKind yields a read_only_review_prompt_reference label only. Human escalation rows return grants_human_approval=false with approval_reference_only=true and persisted=false. Every secondary-workflow and permission/audit-binding kind maps via STATUS_BY_KIND to a blocked_*/ *_not_executed / *_bound status with fail_closed=true and evaluator_invoked=false, covering missing tenant/actor/matter/resource, unknown action, cross-tenant, permission denied, ambiguous/stale, lock conflict, retry exhaustion, rollback, compensation, blocked-claim receipt, fixture, tests, audit hint, and Hermes evidence. Audit/Hermes/blocked-claim outputs are preview/reference-only (emitted_to_audit_ledger=false, writes_hermes_runtime=false, emitted_to_hermes_runtime=false, persisted=false); fixtures/tests are deterministic synthetic metadata (contains_real_data=false, runs_against_synthetic_metadata_only=true, replay_persists_state=false, replay_acquires_lock=false). freezeNoWriteResult forces the full no-write attestation false across all cases (no policy mutation, audit/product/database writes, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, retry/rollback/compensation execution, export/share, AI/analytics, claude review, Hermes runtime, LDIP) and the per-case loop re-asserts these plus unauthorized_count_exposed=false and hidden_field_names_exposed=false. CP125 inheritance is wired through the imported boundary (result_count=10, lock_conflict='blocked_lock_conflict', Hermes reference_only=true). Hidden source fields are declared as names only and never rendered as values; the unknown-case path fails closed to blocked_before_permission_evaluation. The contract JSON's public_exports, hidden_source_fields, and no_write_attestation align with the module constants, and all 11 exports are defined and re-exported via index.js. The diff is pure metadata/catalog/test code with no fs/network/process/eval, no runtime UI/API route, and handoff fixed to CP00-127 / RP02.P07.M05.S17. No P0-P3 issues found.
